Editors

How to get jobs as a video editor

The easiest editor jobs to win are the ones where your portfolio, process, and outreach all match the work the hiring team already needs.

Pick the work you want more of

A broad reel rarely answers the hiring question. Choose a few formats such as YouTube, short-form social, podcast, brand, paid social, event, assistant editor, or local production work and make the proof easy to scan.

Build matched samples

For each target format, keep two or three clips that show pacing, sound, captions, graphics, turnaround, and your exact role. Add short notes so the hiring team knows what you handled.

Make outreach specific

Replace generic messages with a short fit statement: the format you edit, your strongest proof, your availability, and what kind of project you can help with this month.

Join lists that preserve context

A focused editor list can carry more useful signal than a generic profile: portfolio, rates, software, timezone, capacity, turnaround, preferred formats, and source page.

Want better editing work?

Join the editor list and tell us what work fits you.
